events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: this is a **fail**, high risk. Guardrail top rails are engineered only to OSHA 1926.502(b)'s 200 lbf strength test to prevent workers from falling past the perimeter — they are never load-tested or certified as fall-arrest anchorage under 1926.502(d), which requires 5,000 lbf capacity (or engineered design with a 2x safety factor). Task duration doesn't change the physics: an arrested fall would almost certainly exceed the guardrail's capacity and cause anchor failure. The 40-foot distance to the real anchor is a logistics problem (temporary rated anchor, strap, or lifeline) to solve, not a reason to tie off to a non-rated point.
